config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diutamakan tanpa kontrol, mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan atas nama “kebaikan bersama”. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, terutama soal keamanan, kesehatan, dan ketertiban umum, tetapi tidak boleh menghapus martabat dan kebebasan dasar tiap orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dilindungi dengan kuat, lalu kepentingan bersama dicapai tanpa mengorbankan orang yang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should have blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From a United States perspective, I am wary of any argument that lets "the common good" override basic rights too easily, because that has historically been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every case, since public health, safety, and fair laws sometimes require limits, but those limits should be narrow and justified. To me, the best standard is that society can constrain individuals only when it is necessary and proportionate, not because the majority simply wants it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, saya justru melihat betapa mudahnya dalih “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan kelompok kecil, atau mengabaikan orang yang lemah. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tapi pembatasannya harus sangat ketat, jelas alasannya, dan benar-benar perlu, misalnya untuk keselamatan publik atau kesehatan. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan bersama” bisa berubah jadi alat kekuasaan, bukan perlindungan bagi masyarakat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that kind of rule sounds dangerous because “the common good” has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are unlimited; rules for public health, safety, and fair order can be justified when they are narrow, necessary, and proportionate. In my view, society is strongest when it protects individual rights first and only limits them in carefully defined cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan kritik, menutup ruang minoritas, atau membenarkan kebijakan yang merugikan orang kecil. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi hanya kalau benar-benar perlu, jelas dasarnya, dan proporsional, bukan karena mayoritas merasa paling benar. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru lahir ketika hak dasar dilindungi dulu, lalu kepentingan bersama dibangun tanpa mengorbankan martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the claim that society's interests should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that framing is too dangerous because it gives majorities and governments too much room to trample speech, privacy, and minority protections under the banner of the common good. I do think there are real exceptions for public health, safety, and fair laws, but those limits have to be narrow, necessary, and proportionate, not a default assumption that the individual must yield. In my view, a free society earns legitimacy by protecting rights first and only restricting them when there is a clear, defensible need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
